Fusion Antibodies plc
("Fusion"

"Company")
Holdings in Company
Fusion Antibodies plc (AIM: FAB), specialists in pre-clinical antibody discovery, engineering and supply for both therapeutic drug and diagnostic applications, has been informed today that, following a purchase of shares on 29 June 2026, Simon Peace holds 5,121,744 ordinary shares of 4 pence each in the Company, representing approximately 4.09 per cent. of the Company's issued share capital.

Investor Hub
We encourage all investors to share questions on this announcement via our investor hub
https://investorhub.fusionantibodies.com/link/rJaa9e
About Fusion Antibodies plc
Fusion is a Belfast based contract research organisation ("CRO") providing a range of antibody engineering services for the development of antibodies for both therapeutic drug and diagnostic applications.
The Company's ordinary shares were admitted to trading on AIM on 18 December 2017. Fusion provides a broad range of services in antibody generation, development, production, characterisation and optimisation. These services include antigen expression, antibody production, purification and sequencing, antibody humanisation using Fusion's proprietary CDRx TM platform and the production of antibody generating stable cell lines to provide material for use in clinical trials. Since 2012, the Company has successfully sequenced and/or expressed over 750 antibodies and successfully completed over 250 humanisation projects and has an international, blue-chip client base, which has included eight of the top 10 global pharmaceutical companies by revenue.
The Company was established in 2001 as a spin out from Queen's University Belfast. The Company's mission is to enable pharmaceutical and diagnostic companies to develop innovative products in a timely and cost-effective manner for the benefit of the global healthcare industry. Fusion Antibodies provides a broad range of services in antibody generation, development, production, characterisation and optimisation.
Fusion Antibodies growth strategy is based on combining the latest technological advances with cutting edge science to deliver new platforms that will enable Pharma and Biotech companies get to the clinic faster, with the optimal drug candidate and ultimately speed up the drug development process.

30 June 2026
Fusion Antibodies plc
("Fusion"
or the
"Company")
Total Voting Rights
Fusion Antibodies plc (AIM: FAB), specialists in pre-clinical antibody discovery, engineering and supply for both therapeutic drug and diagnostic applications, announces that the issued share capital of the Company at the date of this announcement comprises 125,156,878 ordinary shares of 4 pence each with one voting right per share ("Ordinary Shares"). The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of Ordinary Shares and voting rights in the Company is 125,156,878.
The above figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Notice of allowance of patent in Canada
latest.content:
22 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1820J
Fusion Antibodies PLC
22 June 2026
22 June 2026
Fusion Antibodies plc
("Fusion"
or the
"Company")
Notice of allowance of patent in Canada
Fusion Antibodies plc (AIM: FAB), specialists in pre-clinical antibody discovery, engineering and supply for both therapeutic drug and diagnostic applications, announces
that the Canadian Intellectual Property Office has issued a notice of allowance in respect of the Company's Canadian patent application no. 3,115,150 (the "
Patent Application
").
The Patent Application entitled "Antibody Library and Method", covers two families of antibodies, and the method for the design of such antibody libraries. The patent, once granted, is expected to be complementary to Fusion's offering to provide "Opti" designed antibody libraries for a range of applications including: Antibody Discovery; Affinity Maturation; and Sequence Optimisation.
The Company is also progressing additional patent applications in respect of the OptiMAL® Library in Canada and several other territories worldwide including Europe and China.
Receipt of a notice of allowance indicates that the claims in the Patent Application are patentable and does not in itself represent a grant of patent rights. Fusion anticipates that the patent will be granted upon completion of certain administrative requirements, including payment of the Patent Application, in due course.

latest.title: Update on grant-funded DR5 project
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1836I
Fusion Antibodies PLC
15 June 2026
15 June 2026
Fusion Antibodies plc
("Fusion"
or the
"Company")
Update on grant-funded DR5 project
Project to develop a DR5 humanised therapeutic antibody to combat treatment-resistant cancers
Fusion Antibodies plc (AIM: FAB), specialists in pre-clinical antibody discovery, engineering and supply for both therapeutic drug and diagnostic applications, provides the following update on the progress of the InnovateUK Launchpad grant awarded to the Company in collaboration with Queen's University Belfast (QUB) (the "
Grant
"). The primary purpose of the Grant is to develop a humanised antibody targeting and activating the DR5 protein on cancer cells for the treatment of cancers resistant to other therapies, with the aim of producing a clinic-ready therapeutic asset capable of being licensed to a third-party pharmaceutical or biotechnology company. The Grant funds are being provided by UK Research & Innovation ("UKRI") and InvestNI.
The DR5 antibody has been successfully humanised from the initial rabbit antibody using the Company's proprietary CDRx® humanisation platform, and a number of more complex structural variants have been developed and assessed in parallel. All variants are producing positive results in tumour cell death assays, demonstrating the Company's expertise with advanced antibody engineering. The gene encoding the lead humanised antibody has been successfully inserted and developed into a stable cell line suitable for large-scale manufacture. This cell line development part of the process made use of new state-of-the-art microfluidic technology housed at the Future Medicines Institute and reduced the process timeline by approximately 50 per cent to around four months. This technology is available for use by Fusion Antibodies for client projects and the data will be used as a case study in the Company's marketing activities.
Given the progress made to date, UKRI has agreed to extend the project from its originally targeted completion date of 31 October 2026 to 31 December 2026. Over the remaining months of the project, the DR5 humanised antibody and selected variants will undergo further pre-clinical evaluation in relevant cancer models. Positive results are expected to support a patent application covering the antibody family and could lead to the creation of a licensable therapeutic asset for further development and commercialisation by an industry partner.
In summary, the highlights of the Grant include:
·
Total Grant funding is over £808K of which up to £545K of direct non-dilutive funding available to Fusion
·
Eligible costs include Labour, Overheads and Materials
·
The project has provided valuable Case Study data for several of Fusion's key technologies including:
o
CDRx® humanisation
o
complex protein engineering capability
o
more rapid cell line development
o
the potential to generate a licensable asset
·
A formal Collaboration Agreement has been signed by Fusion and QUB
Dr. Adrian Kinkaid, CEO of Fusion, will present on the DR5 project at the ELRIG Drug Discovery conference in Boston, USA, later this month.
Adrian Kinkaid, CEO of Fusion, said:
"
I am delighted with the Grant support from InnovateUK enabling us to smoothly and successfully progress this project. It shows off several of our comprehensive capabilities in antibody development in a way which can benefit our clients and be used to support our marketing activities. It also demonstrates the value and utility of the Future Medicines Institute in supporting the further development of the capabilities to be found at Fusion in Belfast.
"
Richard Buick, CSO of Fusion, said:
"The DR5 project continues to make excellent progress. The latest variants have shown robust activity in induced cancer cell death assays, supporting their advancement into further studies. With the accelerated process to produce stable cell lines now in place, we are well positioned to deliver a comprehensive data package for the DR5 antibody asset as planned."
Navigate to our Interactive Investor hub here:
https://investorhub.fusionantibodies.com/
. Engage with us by asking questions, watching video summaries and seeing what other shareholders have to say.

latest.title: Exercise of options and total voting rights
latest.content:
2 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5443G
Fusion Antibodies PLC
02 June 2026
2 June 2026
Fusion Antibodies plc
("Fusion"
or the
"Company")
Exercise of options and total voting rights
Fusion Antibodies plc (AIM: FAB), specialists in pre-clinical antibody discovery, engineering and supply for both therapeutic drug and diagnostic applications, announces the exercise of options by an employee of the Company.
The employee has exercised options over 135,000 ordinary shares of 4p each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") at an exercise price of 4.25p per option. To satisfy the exercise the Company is issuing 135,000 new Ordinary Shares (the "New Ordinary Shares"). Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for the New Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission") and it is expected that Admission will be effective, and dealings will commence in respect of the New Ordinary Shares at 8.00 a.m. on or around 8 June 2026.
Following Admission, the Company will have 125,156,878 Ordinary Shares in issue. The Company does not currently hold any shares in treasury and therefore this figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculation by which they may determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: FAB Ltd. builds and sells a proprietary mammalian display platform called OptiMAL, used by biopharmaceutical companies to discover and develop therapeutic antibodies. The platform lets researchers screen large libraries of antibody candidates in human cells, speeding up lead identification.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Antibody therapies are a multi‑billion‑dollar market, and the speed and success rate of discovery are key competitive advantages. OptiMAL offers a unique human‑cell screening step that many competitors miss, giving FAB a potentially high‑margin niche in a growing industry.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early‑stage, pre‑commercial phase. It has raised a modest £1.4m through a share issue, secured grant funding for a research project, and is in the process of transferring key IP. Recent contract wins and the launch of its OptiMAL platform signal that the product is moving toward market fit, but the firm remains cash‑burning with no solid revenue stream yet.

rns_narrative: FAB is actively moving its platform from prototype to market by securing grants, patents and early client contracts. The company is also raising capital to support these steps, indicating a focus on scaling rather than short‑term profitability.

Two major themes emerge: 1) **Capital & IP build‑out** – grant funding, patent approval in Canada and IP transfer agreements show the company is solidifying its technology base. 2) **Early commercial traction** – contract wins with existing clients and negotiations with the National Cancer Institute highlight a growing pipeline of potential revenue.
These signals suggest the company is stabilising its operations and beginning to generate cash‑flow potential, but it still faces significant execution and funding risks.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious – the community is largely silent, with a few isolated bearish posts about share dumps.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter exists. The few positive comments are generic and not tied to any specific event.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A handful of traders note large share sales and the company’s continued cash burn, raising concerns about liquidity and the ability to keep the platform alive.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The overall mood remains flat. There is no sudden optimism, but the lack of noise also means a potential surprise catalyst could go unnoticed.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for FAB is dead, which is a classic contrarian signal – the market is ignoring the stock, so any news or price move could catch traders off guard.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly financial report (likely in Q3 2026) will show whether the company has secured any new contracts or raised additional capital.
A formal partnership announcement with a large pharma or a regulatory filing for the OptiMAL platform would signal commercial viability.
A significant uptick in trading volume (e.g., 5‑10× the 30‑day average) around a price support level could indicate institutional interest picking up.
expect_next.watch_for: Key indicators to monitor over the next 2–3 months are: (1) any new contract signed or renewal announced, (2) a funding round or grant award, (3) a spike in daily volume that breaks a 20‑day moving‑average, and (4) any mention of regulatory milestones in press releases.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early recovery phase of a deep crash – the price has dropped far below recent highs, and volume has thinned out. The APEX compression score suggests a tight range that could act as a spring if a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: FAB’s platform sits in a high‑growth niche with clear IP protection. If the company can convert its early client wins into revenue and secure additional funding, the price could rebound to 50–70% of its pre‑crash level. This would represent a 1.5‑2× return on the current market price. The deep crash also means the downside is already locked in, so the upside is largely asymmetric.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm has no proven revenue and is still cash‑burning. Failure to secure further funding or a key partnership could lead to insolvency. The worst‑case scenario is a continued decline to a liquidation level, wiping out the current investment.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is capped at the current price (the deep crash has already taken the worst case). Upside could be 1.5‑2× if the platform gains traction. The risk‑reward ratio is therefore favourable for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to a recent support level, with a stop 10% below that level. Allocate 10–15% of your discretionary capital to the position, and use a trailing stop to protect gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until either a new contract is announced or a funding round closes. Watch for volume surges that could signal institutional buying.
playbook.if_passing: No clear terminal risk is present, so we do not recommend a passing strategy. The primary risk is that the company fails to secure funding or a commercial partnership.

ai_insights.bottom_line: FAB’s deep crash and silent market create a high‑reward contrarian play – if a funding or partnership win materialises, the price could recover to 50‑70% of pre‑crash levels, delivering a 1.5‑2× upside with the downside already capped.
